metadata.title: Certified Copy of an Entry of Marriage, SZ-AP2/L-S7.9

context:
## Dieses Dokument

- Titel: Certified Copy of an Entry of Marriage
- Signatur: SZ-AP2/L-S7.9
- Datum: Sixth September 1939
- Sprache: Englisch
- Objekttyp: Typoskript
- Umfang: 1 Blatt
- Anmerkungen: Beglaubigte Kopie vom 17. Mai 1940

pages.transcription:
TA 536045
[Printed by Authority of the Registrar-General.]
CERTIFIED COPY of an ENTRY OF MARRIAGE.
Pursuant to the Marriage Acts, 1811 to 1934. M. Cert. S.R.
The Statutory Fee for this Certificate is 2s. 7d. If required subsequently to registration, a Search Fee is payable in addition.
Registration District Bath.
1939 Marriage Solemnized at The Register Office in the County Borough of Bath.
District of Bath
Columns: 1 When Married. 2 Name and Surname. 3 Age. 4 Condition. 5 Rank or Profession. 6 Residence at the time of Marriage. 7 Father's Name and Surname. 8 Rank or Profession of Father.
138 Sixth September 1939 Stefan Zweig 57 years The divorced husband of Friederike Maria Zweig formerly Burger. Author Lansdown Lodge Lansdown Road Bath. Moritz Zweig (deceased) Textile Manufacturer
Elisabet Charlotte Altmann 31 years Spinster Author's Secretary Lansdown Lodge Lansdown Road Bath. Joseph George Altmann (deceased) Hardware Merchant.
Married in the Register Office according to the Rites and Ceremonies of the by licence before me, A. Clegg Registrar
This Marriage was solemnized between us, Stefan Zweig Elisabet Charlotte Altmann in the Presence of us, Arthur I. Ingram Johanna Altmann B. J. Butlin Supt. Registrar
I, W. R. Loxston Deputy Superintendent Registrar for the District of Bath in the County Borough of Bath do hereby certify that this is a true copy of the Entry No. 138 in the Register Book of Marriages No. 96 and that such Register Book is now legally in my custody.
WITNESS MY HAND this 17th day of May, 1940.
W. R. Loxston Deputy Superintendent Registrar
CAUTION.—Any person who (1) falsifies any of the particulars on this Certificate, or (2) uses it as true, knowing it to be falsified, is liable to Prosecution.
pages.notes: Das Dokument ist eine beglaubigte Heiratsurkunde. Die handschriftlichen Einträge sind gut lesbar. Die Briefmarke (Victoria & George 1840-1940) ist unten rechts aufgeklebt.

result.confidence_notes: Der Text ist aufgrund der klaren Struktur und der guten Lesbarkeit der Handschrift sehr gut transkribierbar. Die wenigen Abkürzungen (z.B. 'Supt.') sind eindeutig.
